fact: April 4 is exactly 94 days into the year - that's like counting to 94 Mississippi's without stopping!
In the Northern Hemisphere, tree buds are bursting open right now and some trees grow up to 2 inches in just one week during early April!
Why do mornings feel so crisp around this time? Because April 4 sits right in that sweet spot where night temperatures can still drop 20 degrees below daytime highs.
Earthworms are having a party! They come up to the surface in huge numbers during early April rains, making sidewalks look like spaghetti explosions.
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier each day around April 4, which means by the end of the week, you get 14 extra minutes of daylight!
April 4 falls smack in the middle of Aries season, when the zodiac's ram is supposedly making everyone extra energetic and ready to headbutt their problems.
Dandelions are secretly taking over the world right now - a single dandelion plant can produce 2,000 seeds, and they're all getting ready to fly away on April breezes.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, April 4 marks the time when leaves turn brilliant orange and red, making it look like the trees are on fire.
The moon phase around April 4 changes every year, but when it's a new moon, you can see about 2,500 more stars than during a full moon - that's like having a cosmic light switch!
Migrating butterflies are crossing entire countries right now, with some Painted Lady butterflies flying up to 100 miles per day through early April skies.
April 4 is roughly 2 weeks after the spring equinox, which means daylight is winning the battle against darkness by about 3 minutes every single day.
Frogs are absolutely losing their minds with excitement! Male frogs croak up to 1,000 times per hour during early April mating season - imagine that noise in your backyard!
